description: Bacteriophage phi80 attP attachment site; 480 bp, source-verified 100% to AY048738.1. Recombination substrate for the phi80 tyrosine integrase.

label: phi80_attP
note: Bacteriophage phi80 phage attachment site (attP), 480 bp. Recombination substrate for the phi80 tyrosine integrase (Int) acting with E. coli integration host factor (IHF); recombines with the chromosomal attB site in the E. coli thrW/yciI region to integrate the phi80 prophage (and, in the CRIM system, an attP-bearing plasmid). The 17 bp common core lies near the centre; the flanking P (positions ~279-480) and P' (positions ~0-262) arms carry the Int arm-type and IHF binding sites. Source AY048738.1 is the CRIM integration plasmid pAH162, which carries the phage attP.

label: phi80 crossover (common core)
note: 17 bp phi80 common core / crossover region, the segment shared identically between phi80 attP and the chromosomal attB and across which Int catalyses strand exchange. Read on the minus strand the core is ACACTTTCTTAAATTGT (the plus-strand bases at these coordinates are ACAATTTAAGAAAGTGT). Leong et al. 1985 established the 17 bp core by sequencing phi80 attP and attB; Rotman et al. 2012 independently report the identical 17 bp core (ACACTTTCTTAAATTGT) in the phi80 genome. Experimentally grounded core sequence; the catalog assigns strand -1 to reflect the canonical minus-strand reading.

label: phi80 Int arm-binding site P'1 (candidate)
note: Candidate arm-type integrase binding site in the P' arm. The 9 bp element TGGGTACAC recurs at 83-92 and 103-112 (20 bp centre-to-centre, ~2 helical turns), matching the tandem arm-type architecture of lambda-family attP arms. Assignment rests on sequence inspection plus analogy to lambda arm-type sites (Ross & Landy 1982) and on phi80/lambda arm sequence homology (Leong et al. 1985); NO published phi80 Int footprint exists at these specific coordinates. PROVISIONAL — extent and identity rest on consensus/analogy alone, not direct phi80 evidence.

label: phi80 Int arm-binding site P'2 (candidate)
note: Second candidate arm-type integrase binding site in the P' arm, 20 bp downstream of P'1 (one full repeat unit). Same TGGGTACAC element. Assignment rests on the tandem-repeat structure plus analogy to lambda arm-type sites (Ross & Landy 1982; Landy 1989) and phi80/lambda arm homology (Leong et al. 1985); NO direct phi80 Int footprint at these coordinates is published. PROVISIONAL — extent and identity rest on consensus/analogy alone.

label: IHF binding site (H2 analog)
note: E. coli integration host factor (IHF) binding site in the P arm, the phi80 analog of the lambda H2 site. Leong et al. 1985 demonstrated by DNase I footprinting with purified IHF that phi80 attP carries a single IHF site in the P arm, left of the core in phage convention — so IHF binding in this region is EXPERIMENTAL. The 13 bp window AATCAAAAAGTTA satisfies the IHF consensus WATCAANNNNTTR (Hales et al. 1994); IHF bends the bound DNA >160 degrees via indirect readout (Rice et al. 1996; Robertson & Nash 1988). PROVISIONAL applies only to the precise 13 bp boundaries, which are assigned from consensus matching, not from a published phi80 footprint coordinate.

provenance.sequence_source: AY048738.1 (CRIM plasmid pAH162, Haldimann & Wanner 2001), complement(361..840), 100% over 480 bp

functional_claims.label: Recombination substrate for phi80 integrase acting with E. coli IHF

value.mechanism: phi80 attP is the phage attachment site recombined by the phi80 tyrosine integrase (Int) together with the host integration host factor (IHF). Int and IHF assemble on the attP arms (Int arm-type sites and a single IHF site in the P arm, analogous to lambda H2) to bring the 17 bp common core into the recombinogenic intasome; site-specific strand exchange across the core integrates phi80 (or an attP-bearing plasmid) into the cognate chromosomal attB site.

source.quote: The phi80 and P22 attachment sites: primary structure and interaction with Escherichia coli integration host factor — phi80 attP has a 17 bp common core and a single IHF site in the P arm analogous to lambda H2.

functional_claims.label: 17 bp common core ACACTTTCTTAAATTGT shared with attB

value.note: Identical 17 bp segment in attP and attB; strand exchange occurs within it.

source.quote: phi80 17 bp core sequence ACACTTTCTTAAATTGT at phi80 genome position 24623-24639.

functional_claims.label: Functions for site-specific integration in E. coli

value.context: Validated as an integration site for phi80 Int-mediated chromosomal integration in E. coli, both natively (phi80 prophage) and as the attP element of conditional-replication integration (CRIM) plasmids and one-step cloning-and-integration vectors.

source.quote: Conditional-replication, integration, excision, and retrieval plasmid-host systems for gene structure-function studies of bacteria — CRIM plasmids using lambda and phi80 attP for chromosomal integration in E. coli.

functional_claims.label: Standalone attP for single-copy chromosomal integration

value.application: Deployed as a standalone attP element to direct single-copy integration of a plasmid into the E. coli chromosome at the phi80 attB locus, using a supplied phi80 Int (e.g. CRIM helper plasmids and one-step clonetegration vectors).

source.quote: One-step cloning and chromosomal integration of DNA — phi80 attP deployed as a standalone integration site.
